an5.165:1.1: Then Ven. Sāriputta addressed the monks: “Friend monks.” “Yes, friend,” the monks responded to him. Ven. Sāriputta said: “All those who ask questions of another do so from any one of five motivations.
an5.165:1.3: “One asks a question of another through stupidity & bewilderment. One asks a question of another through evil desires & overwhelmed with greed. One asks a question of another through contempt. One asks a question of another when desiring knowledge.
an5.165:1.4: Or one asks a question with this thought,1 ‘If, when asked, he answers correctly, well & good. If not, then I will answer correctly (for him).’
an5.165:1.5: “All those who ask questions of another do so from any one of these five motivations.
an5.165:1.6: And as for me, when I ask a question of another, it’s with this thought:
an5.165:1.7: ‘If, when asked, he answers correctly, well & good. If not, then I will answer correctly (for him).’
